rationale:
I audited plan v11 against the objective's four tests: implementation-readiness, consistency with spec v2.8, conformance to the repository trust model, and internal coherence of its own decisions and exit criteria. It passes all four.

TRUST-MODEL CONFORMANCE. (1) Fail-closed is pervasive and mechanically enforced, not aspirational: unknown kinds/statuses/schemes reject deterministically; the shallow-repository guard (D16/D18) refuses to weave rather than mint a wrong identity, proven against real git in both directions; nonliteral specifier forms, loader-construction routes (D30/D32), and closure anomalies (D33) all fail closed with per-form synthetic tests. (2) Closed sets are genuinely closed: registries are private-Set read-only facades (not freeze-of-Set illusions), the inventory-id table and LOADER_CAPABLE_BUILTIN_SAFE_EXPORTS mapping are frozen in the plan body with committed-equals-normative unit proofs, and expanding either requires spec amendment, not implementation choice. (3) Spine read-only is explicit, direction-checked (clotho may import merkle-dag, never the reverse), and re-verified in the Task 7 final-diff review. (4) Advisory-only is proven bidirectionally with real-path (not lexical) resolution, symlink rejection, and evasion-route tests (D23/D27).

INTERNAL COHERENCE. The decision set is unusually well cross-stitched. The chronic failure modes of plans like this are absent: (a) the D17/D19 sequencing resolves the bootstrap paradox of inventories naming not-yet-existing files — Task 3 validates structure with injected coverage only, Task 5 discharges inventory-equality in the PR that creates weave.mjs; (b) Task 0-after-Task-1 ordering prevents a knowingly-red workflow PR while keeping the workflow change isolated and human-reviewed; (c) D5/D26/D29 close the self-reported-accounting hole — weavers return only {edges, warnings}, counts are driver-observed through counted iterators, 'executed' means proven complete consumption, and the normative edge-append ordering keeps an incomplete weaver's edges out of the temporary ledger; (d) D20/D28 freeze the publication commit point with exclusive-link semantics, eliminating both the rename-over TOCTOU window and the ambiguity of a failed cleanup (published-cleanup-incomplete is a distinct machine-visible state, never a rollback); (e) D14/D27/D33 close the provenance/enforcement gap by requiring one shared parser, classifier, and resolver for both the outbound scanner and closure derivation, with a unit proving the two consumers share it — a form the scanner permits is a form the closure traverses; (f) D31 removes the hidden doc-weaver-to-ledger-weaver data dependency so skipping one weaver cannot silently starve another; (g) D11/D29 coverage honesty means queries answer coverage-unknown rather than returning silently smaller results, and the verifier's proof boundary is honestly stated (it does not claim to reconstruct runtime iterator exhaustion from the signed ledger).

IMPLEMENTATION-READINESS. Normative material is in the plan body, not delegated: exact locator schemas per kind, the endpoint matrix matching spec v2.3's canonical semantics (including the two-hop discharges walk), the chain/signature byte discipline, the trailer schema with D24's closed inspected_source_counts shape, frozen weaver ids and status couplings, and the per-weaver inventory-id/count-definition table. Task boundaries are single-PR-scoped with explicit exit criteria; the test matrix is enumerated concretely down to individual rejection fixtures (skipped-with-nonzero-count manifests, tampered-inventory omission via re-export/dynamic-import/require routes, publication races, injected unlink failures, symlinked parent components, both verified-by provenance cases per D25).

RISK ACCOUNTING. The 20 accepted risks are each bounded, deliberate, and consistent with the decisions that create them (e.g. abort-on-failure availability is the D15 trade recorded for provenance; conservative closure inflation is honest over-inclusion per D33; the symlink-check residual race is narrowed, acknowledged, and confined to advisory git-ignored output). None of them hides an unbounded or trust-model-violating exposure.

RESIDUAL OBSERVATIONS (none rise to a blocking condition or required edit): the unusual Task 1 → Task 0 numbering is fully explained in-plan; the flagship 120-second wall ceiling is a pragmatic CI bound whose failure mode is a test failure, not a trust breach; cross-ledger adjudication of published artifacts is explicitly out of Phase 1 scope rather than silently unspecified; heading normalization and Markdown section-splitting rules are precise enough to implement without guessing. The plan honestly distinguishes provenance from cryptographic signature for workshop artifacts per its own governance section.

I verified the two governance conditions the objective emphasizes: the plan does not duplicate or modify the governing spec, and every spec-touching decision cites its amendment lineage (v2.2 through v2.8, AM-16 through AM-34). Exit criteria are verifiable from committed bytes (snapshot SHA-256, verification.json, match report, review set) rather than from claims. I would stake this seat's signature on executing v11 as written: approve as-is, no hard stops, no required edits.
